graded_decisions.news_since: Stock flat $297-300; Morgan Stanley Overweight reiteration; iOS Brazil distribution agreement (CNBC/Yahoo, June 2026)
graded_decisions.comment: Exit at $297.59 was well-timed. AAPL barely moved since. No clear near-term catalyst; cash redeployed into QQQ. Not a strong win but a clean exit.

graded_decisions.news_since: QQQ rallied week of June 15 on U.S.-Iran peace deal; 36/46 Nasdaq-100 Q1 2026 earnings beats; AI infrastructure demand intact (eciks.org, June 2026)
graded_decisions.comment: Bought at the intraday high on June 16; QQQ dropped to $722 by June 17 then recovered to $739. AI-led Nasdaq trend thesis is valid but entry was at the daily peak. Slightly underwater, likely to recover.

graded_decisions.news_since: Six consecutive losing days through June 12; FTC investigating cloud/AI/software bundling; Trump executive order on AI software review; MSFT down 24% YTD 2026 (GuruFocus, EBC, TradingKey, June 2026)
graded_decisions.comment: Azure/AI bull thesis structurally intact (Azure +31%, $37B AI run rate, $595+ analyst PT) but FTC probe and AI capex anxiety ($190B) have crushed near-term sentiment. Entry at $425 was too high; stock printed worst weekly performance in 6 years. Near-term thesis invalidated; reassess above $400.

graded_decisions.news_since: May 2026 earnings: revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY), EPS $1.87 vs $1.77 est; PHLX chip index -10% on June 5 sector rout; $25B debt offering completed June 18; consensus Strong Buy (Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool, June 2026)
graded_decisions.comment: Fundamentals exceptional (data center +92% YoY, networking +199%) but stock sold off post-earnings and hit by June 5 chip rout. Down -2.95% from entry but stabilizing near $210. Thesis intact; hold.

graded_decisions.news_since: S&P 500 steady recovery week of June 15-18 despite tech volatility; market closed June 19 for Juneteenth (Alpaca data)
graded_decisions.comment: Only profitable position. Broad-market anchor offsetting tech single-name drawdowns. Entry below the week's closing range; +1.83% unrealized.

patterns: Near-total tech concentration: MSFT+NVDA+QQQ move together. June 5 PHLX chip index -10% hit all positions simultaneously; SPY is the only partial diversifier.

Entries bought into strength not dips: QQQ entered at $742.88 after recovering +5.4% from the June 5 low; MSFT entered around $425 after an AI-driven run to $460. Consistently landing at short-term highs.

Regulatory and macro blindspot in MSFT thesis: FTC cloud bundling probe and Trump AI executive order were circulating risks not incorporated into the entry. Single-narrative entries (AI momentum only) are vulnerable to news-regime shifts.
Sell discipline outperformed buy discipline: AAPL exit at $297.59 was at near-fair-value, the best-timed execution of the week. The buy side (QQQ at the daily high on a down day) was the worst.
suggested_adjustments: Set MSFT invalidation at $370: do not add to the position until price reclaims $400 with above-average volume OR the FTC investigation formally closes.
Require at least one non-tech holding (energy, financials, or healthcare with Nasdaq beta <0.6) to reduce the near-100% QQQ correlation across the book.

Only initiate new longs on days when the target ETF is NOT down >1.5% intraday. QQQ buy on June 16 executed at the daily high before a 1.8% intraday decline; a simple intraday filter would have improved the entry.

Require 2+ independent news sources before executing a sentiment-driven entry; the MSFT AI thesis drew from a single narrative and missed the FTC and capex-anxiety counter-narratives already circulating in financial media.
summary: First review (June 12-18 2026). Portfolio +1.15% vs SPY +0.69% / VT +0.88% / SSO +1.76%. Beat SPY and VT; trailed SSO. Only profitable position is SPY (+1.83%); MSFT is the biggest drag (-10.79%) after FTC probe and AI capex anxiety invalidated the near-term thesis. NVDA mixed (-2.95%), fundamentals intact but sector sell-off. QQQ bought at poor intraday timing, -0.30%. Priority actions: set MSFT stop at $370, diversify away from tech concentration, implement intraday entry filter. SSO note: 2x daily reset yielded +1.76% vs simple 2x SPY of +1.37% -- favorable path (no whipsaw) benefited the leveraged product this week.
